highlights.text: The bull case tomorrow is simply that ES can resume up to backtest 6766 again.
My lean was they could push through.
I wrote at 4pm yesterday: “In the most severe bull case ES will hold above 6766 and flag out above 6772 and below today’s highs.
I’ve frequently talked in this regard about ES cycles between two things: Elevator down sells (these are violent, vertical flushes that cut every support), and short squeezes.
Short squeezes are caused by Failed Breakdowns which is where price sweeps a low, traps shorts, and squeezes.
Those Failed Breakdowns typically target a Back-Test of where ES broke down from to start the elevator down sell.
The last two days were case in point here: On Wednesday, ES got hit with a China headline and went elevator down, selling rapidly 100 points from 6790 down to 6690.
If we are really melting down, the best setup here is to wait for the Failed Breakdown of Sunday’s 6694 daily low.
Ideally wait for 6702 to recover before hopping in here.” We saw exactly this at 2pm Wednesday We flushed 6698 down to 6690, recovered/Failed Breakdown, ripped into the close Wednesday to 6740.
I wrote on Wednesday at 4pm: “At 2pm today, ES put in a Failed Breakdown under 6698 and ripped us higher.
In today’s newsletter I’ll talk this, I’ll do a deep dive into the Failed breakdown that caused Wednesday/Thursday’s 90 point squeeze (this is important to know so you can spot it next time).

post_title: Huge Two Week Pattern Breakout For SPX, But Can It (and Bulls) Survive The Slew of Catalysts Next Week? October 27 Plan
